title: Camps * Families/Friends of People With Cancer - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Camps * Families/Friends of People With Cancer 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community AIDS/HIV Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Families/Friends of People With Cancer Former Foster Children Foster Children Muscular Dystrophy Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CAMP KESEM CAMP KESEM UNIVERSITY OF OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a week-long summer camp for children affected by a parent’s cancer that offers peer-based emotional support. Camp activities include games, arts and crafts, sports, water activities, and outdoor recreation. The camp is staffed by screened and trained college students. Eligibility: Ages 6-18 who have lost a parent to cancer, have a parent undergoing cancer treatment, or whose parent is a cancer survivor. Eligible children may return to Camp Kesem more than once. Children with disabilities and special needs are considered on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 525-0719 Visit Website More Details CANDLELIGHTERS FOR CHILDREN WITH CANCER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides practical and emotional support services to children and their families who have been affected by childhood cancer. Families are served from diagnosis through treatment, and bereavement through monthly support groups, family activities and events, teen groups, emergency funds, scholarships, and food pantries and meal programs at local hospitals. 10070 SW Beaverton-Hillsdale Highway Suite 360 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Children ages 0-21 who currently have or have had cancer and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 719-4244 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CANDLELIGHTERS FOR CHILDREN WITH CANCER CANDLELIGHTERS FOR CHILDREN WITH CANCER
